entry.title: EC Global Assignment and LMS Users (multiple users) - how are you using this ?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have been using LMS for 8 years, and are about to deploy EC.

The concept of "Employee Central Global Assignment / Concurrent Employment in LMS", i really don't see any added value for having multiple users (Home and Host) in the LMS.

Has anyone built an alternative way of managing this ?

Two reasons why I don't like this:

a) our staff need to complete loads of pre-requisite Compliance courses before doing any work. If they are using a concurrent or host user, then the system would not recognize prior learning completions. We could manually use Merge users, but this is not really convincing. Finally, this means doing merge back once a host acc

b) Learning should be focussed on the Person, not on the contact. 

I just read in a SAP article that SAP may be considering implementing a People-Centric view. That would make so much sense.

If you work with the standard multiple users, are you satisfied with this?

Thanks,

 

Kristian

entry.title: Why Data Readiness Is the Biggest Success Factor in SAP S/4HANA Migrations?
entry.content:
What Does Data Readiness Mean in S/4HANA Migration? 

Data readiness refers to the process of preparing enterprise data so that it can be migrated into S/4HANA without introducing operational or reporting issues. 

This preparation typically includes several key activities. 

  1. Data profiling: Organizations analyze legacy datasets to identify duplicates, inconsistencies, and missing values. 
  2. Data cleansing: Incorrect or redundant records are corrected or removed to improve overall data quality. 
  3. Master data harmonization: Customer, vendor, and material records are standardized across business units. 
  4. Validation rule implementation: Business rules confirm that data behaves correctly within S/4HANA structures. 

These steps ensure that migrated data supports operational processes and financial reporting from the moment the system goes live. 

Why Does Poor Data Readiness Cause Migration Delays? 

Many S/4HANA programs encounter delays during testing cycles because legacy data structures are incompatible with the new system. 

Common problems include:

  • duplicate business partner records 
  • inconsistent product classifications 
  • incomplete historical transactions 
  • outdated financial mappings 

When these issues are discovered late in the project timeline, migration teams must pause testing cycles to correct underlying data problems. 

This remediation effort can extend migration timelines and increase project costs. 

Organizations that invest in early data readiness avoid these disruptions by identifying and correcting issues before migration testing begins. 

Which Data Areas Require the Most Preparation? 

Not all enterprise data carries the same level of migration risk. Certain datasets require particularly careful preparation. 

  1. Master Data: Customer, vendor, and material records must be standardized before migration to prevent duplicate or inconsistent entities in S/4HANA. 
  2. Financial Data: Chart-of-account mappings and financial structures must align with S/4HANA requirements to ensure accurate reporting. 
  3. Transactional Data: Open transactions, historical orders, and financial postings must be verified for completeness and accuracy. 
  4. Organizational Structures: Legacy system hierarchies must align with the simplified data model used by S/4HANA. 

Preparing these data domains early significantly improves migration stability. 

Common Data Readiness Challenges 

Organizations often underestimate the complexity of preparing legacy data for migration. 

Typical challenges include:

  • inconsistent data definitions across business units 
  • fragmented ownership of master data domains 
  • lack of automated validation rules 
  • limited visibility into data quality metrics 

Without structured governance frameworks, these challenges can delay migration progress. 

Enterprises increasingly recognize that data readiness is both a technical and organizational initiative. 

Data Readiness Challenges vs Prevention Strategies 

Organizations can mitigate migration risks by addressing common readiness challenges early. 

Data Readiness Challenge Operational Impact Prevention Strategy 
Duplicate master records Transaction errors Master data harmonization 
Incomplete transactional history Reporting gaps Data completeness validation 
Misaligned financial mappings Financial discrepancies Chart-of-account alignment 
Weak validation rules Data integrity issues Automated validation checks 
Fragmented ownership Slow issue resolution Governance framework 

These strategies help organizations move into S/4HANA with greater confidence. 

Case Illustration: Improving Data Readiness Before Migration 

A global manufacturing company preparing for S/4HANA migration encountered several issues during early testing cycles. Customer records were duplicated across regional systems, and inconsistent product classifications caused planning discrepancies. 

Initially, project teams attempted to correct these issues manually during testing cycles. However, the scale of the data environment made this approach inefficient. 

The organization implemented a structured data readiness program that included:

  • comprehensive data profiling across business units 
  • master data harmonization processes 
  • automated validation rules during testing cycles 

Governance-driven frameworks supported by solutions such as DataVapte helped automate validation checks, identify inconsistencies, and track data quality improvements. 

Within several testing cycles:

  • duplicate records were significantly reduced. 
  • reconciliation discrepancies were eliminated. 
  • migration testing stabilized. 

The organization completed its S/4HANA migration with minimal disruption.

How Can Enterprises Improve Data Readiness? 

Organizations preparing for S/4HANA migration can improve data readiness by implementing structured preparation strategies. 

  1. Begin Data Preparation Early: Data readiness initiatives should start well before migration testing begins. 
  2. Establish Data Ownership: Clear ownership ensures that responsible teams maintain data quality across domains. 
  3. Implement Automated Validation: Automated validation rules help detect inconsistencies quickly. 
  4. Monitor Data Quality Continuously: Regular monitoring ensures that improvements remain sustainable. 
  5. Adopt Governance-Driven Migration Tools: Modern governance frameworks—such as DataVapte, support validation, reconciliation, and exception management throughout the migration lifecycle. 

These capabilities help organizations maintain consistent data quality as they transition to S/4HANA. 

Why Data Readiness Enables Long-Term ERP Value 

Beyond migration success, strong data readiness practices provide long-term advantages. 

Organizations benefit from: 

  • improved reporting accuracy 
  • stronger analytics capabilities 
  • reduced operational errors 
  • better regulatory compliance 

Because S/4HANA enables real-time analytics and integrated business processes, maintaining high-quality data becomes even more important after migration. 

Data readiness therefore supports both immediate migration stability and long-term digital transformation. 

Conclusion 

S/4HANA migration programs often focus on infrastructure planning and implementation methodologies. Yet the factor that most frequently determines migration success is the readiness of enterprise data. 

Organizations that migrate legacy datasets without proper preparation risk introducing inconsistencies that disrupt operations and delay system stabilization. 

By investing in structured data readiness, through profiling, cleansing, validation, and governance, enterprises can significantly reduce migration risk and achieve a smoother transition to S/4HANA. 

Governance-driven solutions such as DataVapte further strengthen this process by helping organizations monitor data quality, automate validation checks, and maintain consistent data governance across the migration lifecycle. 

For more insights on SAP data governance and migration strategies, visit:
https://innovapte.com/insights

entry.title: Re: OVA8 - Days Open Items fields aging methodology
entry.content: The fields “NoDays OpenI” and “Days Oldest OpenI” in OVA8 do not count days from the posting date. They are based on the baseline date used for due date calculation. This means they measure days overdue (past due date) rather than days since posting. Open uncleared payment differences are also considered open items unless explicitly excluded by customizing or user exits.

entry.title: Re: External Sales Commission - Single Sales Deal
entry.content: Yes, an accrual account key is normally required, but in Public Cloud only certain predefined keys and condition types are settlement relevant. If RCD2 is not mapped to business volume relevance, settlement will never pick it up; regardless of account key. The next step is to check the settlement log and confirm whether RCD2 is supported; if not, you’ll need to use a delivered commission condition type or raise a request with SAP.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Connector installer interrupted
entry.content:
I've had the same issue just today when installing the newest version of Cloud Connector 2.19.0.2 on Windows Server. 

The installer did not extract the com.sap.scc.zip-2.19.0.2-win64-msi.zip file (which is included in the .msi file) which leads to the error

Error: Unable to access jarfile configurator.jar ExecuteCommand(): Execution ended with return code 1.

In the com.sap.scc.zip-2.19.0.2-win64-msi.zip file is included the configurator.jar file - and of course, it cannot be accessed by the java command "java.exe -jar configurator.jar -upgrade" if it is not present unzipped in the installation folder. During installation, I manually extracted the zip-file into the installation directory (this should normally be done by the installer) and after that the installer ended successfully. 

Regards
Christian

entry.title: Isn't RAP overengineered ?
entry.content:
Isn't RAP totally over overengineered and almost turned into a tool for creating Rube Goldberg systems?

How would you make a small report in ABAP Cloud?

When I started programming ABAP I could make a report to list customers in 2 minutes like this.

REPORT ZLIST_CUSTOMERS.
Tables: kna1.
SELECT-OPTIONS s_mcod1 for kna1-mcod1.
select * from kna1
 where mcod1 in s_mcod1.
  write: / kna1-kunnr, kna1-name1.
endselect.

The users kun run the report from SA38 or I could spend additional 2 minutes to create a transaction code.

With RAP I could spend hours creating a RAP application for the same output and I have to create so many objects that I for sure will make some typing errors during the process. 

So now I need the help of Joule to create all the objects like this:

Joule create more objects than I used to have code lines in one object and all I get is still just a list of customers.
I know that RAP can make much more advanced apps, but often I don't need more.

On top of all this I probably have to create an additional bunch of objects to make the report appear in Build Workzone. 

Best regards 
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Re: Number Ranges for SD invoices
entry.content: In SAP SD, billing document number ranges are defined at the level of the billing document type, not directly per company code. Each billing type can only be assigned one number range object, and that object can contain multiple numeric intervals. Could you please confirm whether your requirement is to have separate number ranges per company code, or simply to ensure that each billing type used in those company codes issues invoices with 10‑digit numeric sequences (without letters)?

entry.title: Re: Production process mapping from Finished good (Varian...
entry.content:
Below are the recommended approaches

  • Step 1: Create production order for HRC_SCRAP against dummy scrap sales order.
  • Step 2: Assign HRC_F as BOM component in the order.
  • Step 3: Confirm production order (CO11N).
    • System consumes HRC_F (batch managed).
    • System produces HRC_SCRAP (non-batch managed).
    • Accounting entries:
      • Cr Inventory HRC_F (MAP).
      • Dr Inventory HRC_SCRAP (Standard).
      • Difference → PRD.
  • Step 4: Scrap material HRC_SCRAP is now available for SMS shop consumption.

entry.title: Re: MIRO not calculating taxes (TAXBRA) – Invoice shows p...
entry.content:
Answer to your questions:

  1. Yes, this is related to missing LIV tax determination configuration.
  2. PO tax conditions (TAXBRA) must be linked to MIRO via tax code.
  3. The tax code should be copied automatically from the PO; manual entry is not expected.
  4. Brazil localization settings (CFOP, tax code, condition mapping) directly affect MIRO behavior.

Why this happens is because

1) PO vs. MIRO behavior:
In ME21N, TAXBRA determines taxes correctly based on CFOP, tax code, and condition records. In MIRO, the system expects the tax code to be carried over from the PO. If the tax code is missing or not copied, MIRO does not calculate taxes.

2) Gross amount entry:
Entering gross value in MIRO does not trigger tax determination. The system balances only if the tax code is present.

3) Brazil localization dependency:
TAXBRA requires correct CFOP, tax code, and condition setup. If the tax code is not mapped to LIV, MIRO will not propose taxes.

Key Configuration areas to check

1) Tax Code Copying
Ensure the tax code is maintained in the PO item and is copied into MIRO. In Brazil localization, the tax code is mandatory for LIV tax determination.

2) Condition Mapping
Verify that the PO tax conditions (ICMS, IPI, ISS, etc.) are linked to the tax code used in MIRO. Without this mapping, MIRO cannot calculate taxes.

3) LIV Settings
Check customizing in Logistics Invoice Verification → Incoming Invoice → Taxes. Confirm that the tax procedure TAXBRA is active for LIV and that the relevant tax codes are assigned.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Angel Aradhana, and I am excited to begin my journey on SAP.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Angel Aradhana , and I’m currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I am very happy to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem and connect with professionals around the world.

As a student, I am interested in learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage their operations efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I hope to learn from experts, discover new ideas, and grow my professional network.

I look forward to sharing my learning journey and contributing to the  SAP  Community in the future.

Thank you SAP Community for this wonderful opportunity!

 

 

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative.

1. What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to build a successful career in technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve their efficiency. I also aim to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled professional in the technology industry. I aim to develop strong technical knowledge, gain hands-on experience, and contribute to real-world projects. I also aspire to continuously learn, grow professionally, and make a meaningful contribution to my organization and community

3. What is your hobby?

One of my hobbies is learning about new technologies and exploring new ideas related to engineering and innovation. I also enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and improving my knowledge through different educational platforms.

4. Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

There are several goals I want to achieve in my life:

Build a successful professional career.
Continuously learn and develop new skills.
Contribute to society through knowledge and innovation.
Inspire others through my journey and experiences.


5. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

The best lesson my parents taught me is the importance of hard work, honesty, and patience. They always encouraged me to stay focused on my goals and never give up during difficult situations. These values continue to guide me in my education and future career.

 

 

#HelloSAPcommunity #SAPcommunity #SAPLearning #SAPForBeginners #TechCommunity #FutureWithSAP

entry.title: Re: Mass download sales order form and invoice form
entry.content: One option is to use transaction RSNAST00 (classic output processing) or the equivalent in Output Management. You can schedule a job to process multiple output requests at once, generating PDFs for all selected documents. The PDFs can be stored in the SAP spool or sent to an external archive.

entry.title: Evaluated Receipt Settlement - manual invoice posting in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts!
We are trying to process invoices for self-billing based on ERS process on SAP Public Cloud system.
Whole process is already configured and working, unfortunately we have some cases which we cannot invoice properly.
For some of materials, due to legal tax requirements, we have to adjust Tax Reporting Date on ERS invoice to a different date, other than posting or document date.

We could modify BADI MRM_ERS_DATA_MODIFY_CE, but tax reporting date is not available at all.
We have tried to process invoice with API Supplier Invoice - OData V2, but it is not supporting ERS invoices completely.

What we are thinking of is to create those invoices manually, maybe with MIRO/Create Supplier invoice - Advanced, but there is no  ERS indicator to flag an invoice as self-billing invoice. Because of that, system is not treating manual invoice as ERS invoice and there is no proper output of the invoice and it is not treated as e-Invoice (i.e. for KSeF purposes).

Is there any, totally manual way, to create such invoice without background job defined in Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs - Advanced?
Maybe someone already struggled with this issue or have an idea how to solve it?

Thanks in advance for all support.
Grzegorz

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! I'm Pati Pradeep Babu
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! I’m Pati Pradeep Babu, currently in my final year at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering and Technology. I still remember the first time I heard about SAP and realized it was the 'brain' behind almost every product I use daily. That realization sparked a deep passion in me for learning how these complex business processes work. As a newcomer, I’m excited to share my learning journey, connect with experts, and grow alongside this amazing community!

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the HelloSAPCommunity  initiative .

What is your professional life dream?  

My professional dream is to become a Certified SAP FICO Consultant at a globally recognized organization. Beyond just mastering the technical configurations of Finance and Controlling, I aim to be a bridge between complex business challenges and streamlined digital solutions. I want to help businesses achieve financial transparency and efficiency, ultimately contributing to the digital transformation of the industry while continuously evolving as an expert in the SAP ecosystem.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?  

The most realistic lesson I learned from my parents is that failure isn't the opposite of success; it’s a part of it. They never pressured me to be perfect, but they always insisted I 'get back up.' As a final-year student stepping into the professional world, that gives me the confidence to take on the steep learning curve of SAP without the fear of making mistakes because I know that’s how I’ll eventually become an expert.

What is my hobby?

Yoga: I’ve been practicing Hatha Yoga for years, and it’s become a huge part of my life—I’ve actually been fortunate enough to win 36 medals in various competitions! But for me, it’s not about the medals anymore; it’s about the mental discipline. In a field as complex as SAP, that ability to stay calm and focused is my secret weapon.

Swimming: I’ve always loved being in the water, and these days, I spend time as a Swimming Trainer. There is something incredibly rewarding about helping someone overcome their fear of water and master a new skill. It’s taught me a lot about patience and how to explain difficult things in simple ways.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore experience

I had the chance to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2026, and 'thrilling' is the only word that truly describes it. Being in a room filled with SAP experts, developers, and fellow students from all over was a massive eye-opener for me.

 

It wasn’t just about the sessions; it was about the energy of seeing how people exchange ideas and solve real-world problems. Seeing the latest advancements in AI and S/4HANA firsthand turned the concepts I’ve been studying into a living, breathing reality. It confirmed for me that I’m exactly where I’m supposed to be."

 
Main Highlights and SAP TechEd Key Takeaways
The event wasn't just about watching presentations; it was about the "aha!" moments that sharpened my practical understanding. Here are my key takeaways:
  • AI-Driven Roadmap: I attended immersive sessions on SAP’s AI-driven strategy and cloud innovations, which showed me how automation is transforming financial transparency.
  • Hands-on Labs: These sessions strengthened my technical skills in SAP integration and core business processes, moving me closer to my goal of professional certification.
  • Inspiring Connections: Having real conversations with the professionals who are driving digital transformation today boosted my confidence and motivated me to build smarter, sustainable enterprise solutions.
Walking out of SAP TechEd, I feel more empowered than ever to take the next steps in my tech journey. It wasn’t just an event—it was a turning point.
 
And now let’s hear from you!
I am excited to apply these insights and grow into a professional capable of building the future of enterprise technology. But before I go, I want to learn from this amazing community
 
"What was the one 'aha!' moment or specific event that started your own SAP journey?" Please share your stories in the comments section below!

Our client will be using Basic TM only. Currently they are not using LE-TRA. They will be directly using Basic TM (embedded S/4 HANA). I have gathered the following requirments

entry.title: ABAP Performance Tuning in S/4HANA Series : Part 4 : ATC
entry.content:
ATC: Make the ABAP Test Cockpit Your Pre-Transport Quality Gate

Series: ABAP Performance Tuning in S/4HANA | Part 4 of 7 | Author: Manish Khanna

Someone transported a SELECT inside a loop to production. It ran fine in the 200-record test system. In production, with 2 million records, it ran for 6 hours and locked the batch window. A 30-second ATC check before transport would have caught it. This post is about making sure that scenario never happens in your landscape.

ATC — ABAP Test Cockpit — is SAP's official static code analysis framework. It ships with the ABAP platform, runs in ADT and SE80, and has a REST API for CI/CD pipelines. It replaces every custom static analysis report your team has built.

What ATC Does

ATC analyzes ABAP source code without executing it. It finds:

 

Check CategoryExamples
PerformanceSELECT *, SELECT in LOOP, missing WHERE clause, FAE without IS NOT INITIAL guard
SQLExpensive SQL patterns, full table scans (when DB metadata available)
SecuritySQL injection risks, missing authority checks, hardcoded credentials
Robust CodeDead code, unreachable statements, potential runtime errors
ABAP Cloud ReadinessDeprecated statements, non-released APIs (for BTP/S/4 migration)
NamingZ/Y naming convention violations

The critical difference from tracing: you don't need representative data, you don't need a trace file, and it takes seconds not minutes.

Priority Levels — What Requires Action

 

Priority Label What It MeansWhat You Must Do
1ErrorCritical — transport should be blockedFix before transport. No exceptions.
2WarningSignificant riskFix, or create an approved exemption
3InformationSuggestionFix at discretion

Priority 1 findings are not negotiable. If you're releasing transports with Priority 1 ATC findings, you are shipping known defects.

Key Performance Checks in Detail

These are the checks you care about most. Priority 1 configuration is recommended for all of them:

 

Check What It Catches Classic Anti-Pattern
SELECT *Fetches all columnsSELECT * FROM vbak INTO TABLE lt_orders
SELECT in LOOPN+1 patternLOOP AT lt. SELECT SINGLE ... ENDLOOP.
FAE without IS NOT INITIALEmpty driver → full scanSELECT ... FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_empty
BYPASSING BUFFERUnnecessary cache bypassSELECT ... BYPASSING BUFFER FROM t001
COMMIT WORK in LOOPTransactional integrity riskLOOP AT lt. UPDATE db. COMMIT WORK. ENDLOOP.
NEW / CREATE OBJECT in LOOPMemory + CPU overheadLOOP AT lt. lo = NEW zcl_proc(). ENDLOOP.

Running ATC Checks

From ADT (Eclipse) — Recommended

  1. Right-click your object or package in the Project Explorer
  2. Run As → ABAP Test Cockpit
  3. Select your check variant (more on variants below)
  4. Results appear in the ATC Results view, grouped by priority

From SE80 (SAP GUI)

  1. Transaction ATC
  2. Enter your program name or package
  3. Select check variant
  4. Execute (F8)
  5. Results grouped by message priority

💡Tip: Always run at package level before transport, not just on the changed object. Downstream programs that consume your changed code can also be affected.

Check Variants — Configure Your Quality Bar

The default ATC variant checks everything at default priorities. That's too noisy for daily use and not strict enough for transport gates.

Create project-specific variants in ATC_CONFIG (or via ADT preferences):

Z_PERF_STRICT — for transport gates:

  • All PERF and SQL category checks at Priority 1
  • Block transport on any Priority 1 finding
  • Zero tolerance for SELECT in LOOP, SELECT *, FAE guard, COMMIT WORK in LOOP

Z_PERF_STANDARD — for development reviews:

  • Performance checks at Priority 2
  • Warning only — does not block transport
  • Useful for daily development feedback

Z_CLOUD_READY — for S/4HANA migration projects:

  • ABAP Cloud readiness checks enabled
  • Catches deprecated statements and non-released APIs

Exemptions — When You Can't Fix It Right Now

Sometimes a Priority 2 finding is a known technical limitation that can't be fixed immediately. ATC has a built-in exemption workflow:

  1. Developer requests exemption in ADT or SE80
  2. Justification text is required — no blank exemptions
  3. Approver (architect or lead) reviews and approves/rejects
  4. Approved exemptions are stored and auditable
  5. Set an expiry date on all exemptions

⚠️Warning: Exemptions bypass the quality gate. Audit your exemption list quarterly. Expired exemptions that were "temporary 18 months ago" are technical debt you forgot about.

CI/CD Integration — The Right Way

If you're using abapGit, ADT, or the SAP CI/CD service, integrate ATC as a pipeline step:

Developer commits code
    ↓
Git push → CI pipeline triggers
    ↓
1. abapGit pull to ATC system
2. ATC run via REST API — check variant Z_PERF_STRICT
   POST /sap/bc/adt/atc/runs
3. Parse JSON results
4. Priority 1 findings > 0?
   → YES: FAIL pipeline. Block merge. Notify developer.
   → NO: Continue
5. Priority 2 findings > 0?
   → YES: WARNING. Notify reviewer.
   → NO: PASS. Allow merge and transport.

The REST API endpoint: POST /sap/bc/adt/atc/runs Authentication: Basic Auth or OAuth2 with a dedicated technical user. Response: XML (ADT format) or JSON — parse the priority and message fields.

This means no Priority 1 performance defect can reach production without a human decision to override the pipeline. That's the quality gate.

ATC vs. Custom Static Analysis Tools

If your team has built a custom ABAP report to scan for performance anti-patterns (25 rules, custom scoring, manual maintenance), here's the honest comparison:

 

Capability Custom ToolATC
Rule updatesManual development effortSAP delivers with kernel/basis updates
IDE integrationUsually SE38 onlyADT, SE80, BAS — full IDE support
CI/CDTypically noneREST API, pipeline-ready
Check scopeWhatever you codedPerformance + security + quality + cloud (hundreds of checks)
Multi-line statement analysisOften single-line scannerFull ABAP parser
Exemption workflowNoneBuilt-in approval + audit trail
Transport blockingNoneConfigurable transport release check

Retire your custom scanner. Maintain your check variants in ATC instead.

Recommended Workflow

  1. Run ATC with Z_PERF_STRICT before raising a transport request
  2. Fix all Priority 1 findings — mandatory
  3. Fix Priority 2 findings or create approved exemptions with documented justification
  4. If the change touches CDS views: run CDS-specific ATC checks too
  5. After ATC is clean: proceed to ST05/SAT trace with representative data

ATC is Step 0, not an optional extra. It saves hours of trace analysis by eliminating the basics upfront.

 

 

Key Takeaways

  • ATC is SAP's official static analyzer — it ships with the platform, no custom tooling needed
  • Priority 1 findings must be fixed before transport. No exceptions.
  • Create project-specific check variants (Z_PERF_STRICT) — don't rely on the default
  • Integrate ATC into your CI/CD pipeline via the REST API (/sap/bc/adt/atc/runs)
  • Audit exemptions quarterly — stale exemptions are hidden technical debt

Next in this series: S/4HANA Performance: CDS Views, Pushdown, and Entity Buffers — why "just move to HANA" is not a performance strategy, and what CDS pushdown actually means in practice.

Series Home Page

entry.title: Re: Manager Rating not available in the summary section a...
entry.content:
Hi Meera @sysfpathforward 

The KBA that you mention was about the Manager Rating and Gap columns missing but not specific to the signature step. This is fixed and those columns are visible, but as long as the employee is able to access ratings from others.

In Latest Experience for Performance Management forms, the side-by-side display is not enabled by default to those users that typically cannot access Gap Analysis. See https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-performance-and-goals/implementing-and-managing-performance-management/ez-rater-latest-form-experience

Another thing to consider is that the Summary section will not show these differences, since the summary will include the official ratings, and the side-by-side ratings are shown at the section level.

Try changing the sect-mode="EZ-Rater" for the goal section and verify there are no restrictions for the Employee to see other's ratings (this is controlled via tab-permissions at section level or in General Settings > Other's Rating Tab Permissions if defined at the template level), as well as no restrictions to view manager rating (item-rating).

Hope this will help to resolve it, 

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Unmanaged scenario example-Simplified
entry.content:
First of all thank you so much @anjan_paul for sharing this. As I am new to RAP and currently learning so having a proper step by step guide helps. 

Though your content is great but it misses some key areas/codes and steps and neither there is any mention of previous blog which can be referred to cover the gaps. So, it creates confusion and frustration for the first timers trying it hands on.

Hey @Jayasurya11 and for anyone else who are trying to implement the example:

In the behavior implementation class, you can add this code for the buffer class -

This is the Buffer Class DEFINITION - Here all the methods which have been used in the further method implementations has been mentioned.
Kindly replace the xx in ZXX_employee with your desired value.

CLASS lcl_buffer DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS get_instance
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_buffer) TYPE REF TO lcl_buffer.

    METHODS set_create_value
      IMPORTING is_data TYPE zxx_employee.

    METHODS set_update_value
      IMPORTING is_data TYPE zxx_employee.

    METHODS set_delete_value
      IMPORTING iv_empid TYPE zxx_employee-empid.

    METHODS get_records
      IMPORTING iv_empid           TYPE zxx_employee-empid
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_employee) TYPE zxx_employee.

    METHODS get_create_value
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_employee) TYPE zxx_employee.

    DATA mt_create TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zxx_employee.
    DATA mt_update TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zxx_employee.
    DATA mt_delete TYPE RANGE OF zxx_employee-empid.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA go_instance TYPE REF TO lcl_buffer.
ENDCLASS.

  Buffer Class Implementation:

CLASS lcl_buffer DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS get_instance
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_buffer) TYPE REF TO lcl_buffer.

    METHODS set_create_value
      IMPORTING is_data TYPE zxx_employee.

    METHODS set_update_value
      IMPORTING is_data TYPE zxx_employee.

    METHODS set_delete_value
      IMPORTING iv_empid TYPE zxx_employee-empid.

    METHODS get_records
      IMPORTING iv_empid           TYPE zxx_employee-empid
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_employee) TYPE zxx_employee.

    METHODS get_create_value
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_employee) TYPE zxx_employee.

    DATA mt_create TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zxx_employee.
    DATA mt_update TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zxx_employee.
    DATA mt_delete TYPE RANGE OF zxx_employee-empid.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA go_instance TYPE REF TO lcl_buffer.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_buffer IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_instance.
    IF go_instance IS NOT BOUND.
      CREATE OBJECT go_instance.
    ENDIF.
    ro_buffer = go_instance.
  ENDMETHOD.

  "------------------------------------------
  " Creates the Buffer
  "------------------------------------------
  METHOD set_create_value.
    INSERT is_data INTO TABLE mt_create.
  ENDMETHOD.

  "------------------------------------------
  " Updates the Buffer
  "------------------------------------------
  METHOD set_update_value.
    INSERT is_data INTO TABLE mt_update.
  ENDMETHOD.

  "------------------------------------------
  " Deletes the Buffer
  "------------------------------------------
  METHOD set_delete_value.

    DATA ls_range LIKE LINE OF mt_delete.

    ls_range-sign   = 'I'.
    ls_range-option = 'EQ'.
    ls_range-low    = iv_empid.

    INSERT ls_range INTO TABLE mt_delete.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_create_value.

    READ TABLE mt_create INTO rs_employee INDEX 1.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_records.

    "Read existing DB record
    SELECT SINGLE *
      FROM zxx_employee
      WHERE empid = @iv_empid
      INTO @rs_employee.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Also even after doing everything correctly, you may get a short dump while trying to see the data in Fiori as soon as you hit that Go Button.

To fix that kindly add this method inside the handler class definition which would be sitting at the top inheriting from the cl_abap_behavior_handler.

    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations 
         FOR employee RESULT result.

Combined together your handler class definition will look like this:

CLASS lhc_employee DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE employee.

    METHODS delete FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR DELETE employee.

    METHODS update FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE employee.

    METHODS lock FOR LOCK
      IMPORTING keys FOR LOCK employee.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ employee RESULT result.

    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR employee RESULT result.

ENDCLASS.

Also, I am open for any feedback that the community may want to give. 

Happy Learning.

entry.title: Error FZ003 - Autopayment
entry.content:
• Document Data Verification (FB03):

    • Payment Method 'S' is correctly entered at the line item level.

    • Payment Block is empty.

    • Baseline Date is today (March 23rd) and Payment Terms are 'ZB00' (Due Immediately), so they meet the due date conditions.

  • F110 Date Parameters:

    • Just in case the Entry Date (CPUDT) was causing an issue, I set both Docs entered up to and Next p/date generously to tomorrow's date (March 24th).

  • FBZP Minimum Amount Check:

    • Checked Pmt methods in company code and confirmed the minimum amount for outgoing payments is 2.50 EUR. My missing invoices are 30 EUR and 40 EUR, so they are not restricted by this limit.

  • Ghost Proposal/Lock Cleared:

    • Suspecting that a previous failed proposal might have locked the documents, I completely deleted the old proposals (Edit -> Proposal -> Delete) and ran it with a new Run ID, but the new documents are still missing.

entry.title: Learn what makes your business work anywhere with SAP’s mobile apps, AI, and SAP BTP
entry.content:
SAP mobile apps with Joule integrationAbout 230 million cloud users worldwide use SAP solutions, running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). SAP's data and business process expertise is unparalleled. However, the possibilities to benefit from this business power everywhere using mobile consumer devices are far from being exhausted by all customers.

A new course on learning.sap.com shows in an easily comprehensible way how to get started with mobile apps from SAP for smart phones, tablets, watches, or mixed-reality headsets.

Discovering SAP's Mobile Experiences is the first version of a learning journey that illustrates why the SAP Mobile Start app and SAP's AI Copilot Joule are excellent entry points for SAP users in many business environments for using powerful features on simple-to-use mobile devices.

The approximately one-hour-long training offers an overview of all available mobile apps provided by SAP for its solutions, and how managing as well as developing further native apps based on SAP BTP works.

Challenges for businesses, opportunities for mobile applications

With a well-orchestrated mobile setup, getting daily tasks done becomes faster and easier for roles such as supply chain managers, mechanics, engineers, finance managers, and many more. Especially in challenging environments, SAP mobile apps accompany employees with deep AI integration and supporting features for simplified and hands-free operation. With additional development possibilities on SAP BTP, customers or partners can extend what’s already been offered by SAP and even build their own app suites, massively simplified and accelerated by the platform's AI coding capabilities.

All of this, shows the training, makes SAP’s mobile offerings a vital part of any digital transformation strategy.

The first version of Discovering SAP's Mobile Experiences provides separate lessons for the powerful SAP Mobile Start and SAP Service and Asset Manager apps. Further chapters on mobile native apps for business travelers, HR, finance will follow soon.

Discovering SAP's Mobile Experiences is publicly available now and soon also accessible via sap.com/mobile.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Papasani Thrisha and excited to Start my SAP Learning journey!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! I’m Papasani Thrisha, a final-year BTech student in Computer Science and Engineering at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering and Technology. I’m fascinated by how businesses manage operations, supply chains, and enterprise processes using SAP solutions.
During my studies, I have explored various SAP concepts, including SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and business process management. I enjoy learning how these technologies can improve efficiency, streamline operations, and enable data-driven decision-making.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I hope to work with global organizations as an SAP professional, gaining expertise in multiple modules and contributing to impactful digital transformation projects.

Hobbies and interests
Apart from my professional interests, I enjoy reading about emerging technologies, exploring enterprise systems, and staying updated on industry trends.  I enjoy exploring new places and also like spending time on sports, music, which helps me maintain a balanced lifestyle.

What is the best lesson my parents taught me? 

Among all the lessons my parents shared with me, the most impactful one was to respect every individual equally.

They taught me that true character is reflected not in how we treat people who are above us, but in how we treat everyone—regardless of their role, background, or identity.

This perspective has deeply influenced my mindset. It has encouraged me to look beyond external differences and value people for who they are.

In many ways, this lesson has shaped not just my behavior, but also my outlook on life—helping me become more empathetic, respectful, and mindful in my interactions.

What is my personal life dream? 

My personal life dream is closely connected with both gratitude and growth.

On a personal level, I aspire to take my parents on trips across the country and around the world—especially my mother, who truly loves to travel. Being able to give them those experiences would mean a lot to me, as they have always supported me throughout my journey.

From a professional perspective, I dream of building a strong career in the SAP ecosystem, where I can contribute to creating intelligent and impactful enterprise solutions. I want to be part of a journey where technology—especially SAP, AI, and cloud—helps businesses grow while also improving people’s everyday lives.

Another dream that is very close to my heart is to give back to society. I hope to use both my personal efforts and professional success to contribute meaningfully—such as providing meals to old age homes and orphanages, and supporting initiatives that create real social impact.

For me, success is not just about achieving career milestones in SAP, but also about using that success to create meaningful experiences for my family and make a positive difference in society.

What do I imagine or predict is the future of AI?

I imagine a future where AI becomes a natural part of our everyday lives.

In daily life, AI could help manage traffic without signals, make homes smarter by cleaning automatically, and even create products that adjust to our needs, like smart footwear.

In the world of technology and SAP, AI will help businesses work more efficiently by automating tasks, analyzing data, and supporting better decision-making.

Overall, I believe AI will make life easier, faster, and more efficient, while helping both individuals and organizations grow.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience

Attending SAP TechEd in Bangalore was a truly inspiring experience.

It gave me the opportunity to interact with industry experts, attend hands-on sessions, and explore how SAP is integrating AI, cloud, and modern technologies into enterprise solutions.

One of the most memorable aspects was the supportive and approachable nature of the SAP community. I also had the opportunity to attempt a Generative AI certification during the event, which was a great learning experience.

 

 The event also gave me the opportunity to expand my network, learn directly from industry experts, and gain a clearer perspective on the future of enterprise technology. 

 I was also introduced to the SAP Community, a platform where individuals share not only technical knowledge but also their hobbies, interests, and personal experiences. It was Moshe Naveh who introduced a group of us students to this community and encouraged us to actively contribute. His encouragement inspired me to write my first SAP Community blog, where I shared my thoughts on emerging technologies and their impact on society.

Attending SAP TechEd and being part of the SAP Community has been an incredible learning experience. I gained hands-on insights into emerging technologies, learned from industry experts, and connected with like-minded peers.

This journey has inspired me to continue exploring SAP, contribute to the community, and apply what I learn to real-world challenges.

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

What is your personal life dream? 

I would love to hear your thoughts—feel free to share them in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Step by Step Implementation of Secure Login Service for SAP GUI - BTP SSO
entry.content:
Hi @sajaysurya7,

We are implementing SAP GUI SSO using SAP BTP Secure Login Service, SAP Cloud Identity Services, and Okta as the corporate IdP. During setup, we encountered two blocking issues:

  1. Metadata option is not visible in Secure Login Service (SLS).
    SLS does not provide SAML metadata and relies on certificates as described in SAP documentation. 

  2. Cloud Identity Services UI does not match IAS documentation, so we cannot locate SAML metadata required for configuring Okta.
    Available SAP blogs reference IAS for SAML trust setup with Okta. 

We followed SAP community implementation guides for SLS, trust setup, certificate import, and Secure Login Client configuration. 

Could you please provide updated SAP standard documentation or SAP Notes for SAP GUI SSO using Okta + Cloud Identity Services + SLS.

Regards,

Vignu Daniels.

entry.title: Enterprise Service Organization Determination in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Overview: Moving Beyond Basic Defaulting

SAP S/4HANA offered a simple defaulting mechanism for Enterprise Service Organizations (ESO), primarily based on sales area mapping to ESO (1:1) with limited flexibility. The new enhancement addresses this limitation by introducing a comprehensive, rule-based determination framework that provides unprecedented control over how service organizations are assigned to service transactions. 

This feature is available when the Enterprise Organizational Model is enabled in the system. 

Disclaimer: Images and data in this blog are from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental. 

Supported Service Transactions 

  1. Service Order/Quotation 
  2. Service Confirmation 
  3. Service Request 
  4. Service Contract: This can be enabled by implementing SAP Note 3708779 

Additionally, please implement the mandatory SAP Note 3708274 to ensure this feature functions correctly. 

Key Benefits

1. Flexible Determination Framework

The enhanced solution provides a customizable framework that determines Enterprise Service Organizations based on multiple data sources:

  • Business Add-Ins (BAdI) for custom logic implementation.
  • Sales Area: Determine the ESO based on the configured mapping between the Sales Area and the ESO.
  • Service Team: Determine the ESO assigned to the service team.

2. Customer-Specific Extensibility

Organizations can now integrate their unique business logic by implementing the new BAdI CRMS4_ESO_DETERMINATION (Single Use), ensuring the determination process aligns seamlessly with their specific business requirements.  

While the existing BAdI CRM_ORGMAN_BADI already supports enterprise service organization determination, the new BAdI introduces significantly enhanced capabilities, such as the ability to trigger determination based on any segment data change and full control over the execution sequence.  

3. Seamless Coexistence

The new functionality works seamlessly with existing manual input capabilities and the sales area-based defaulting logic (which determines the Enterprise Service Organizational based on the configured mapping between the Sales Area and the Enterprise Service Organizational), ensuring there is no disruption to current operations.  

In short, sales area-based defaulting continues to function whenever at least one Enterprise Service Organizational Profile is not configured. This means that when an Enterprise Service Organizational Profile is assigned to the transaction type and at least one determination rule is defined for it (regardless of whether any conditions are maintained), only the new determination logic will function. Otherwise, the system will continue to apply the standard sales area–based defaulting. 

Enterprise Service Organization Profile - What Is It? 

At the core of this enhancement is the new Enterprise Service Organization Profile. This configurable profile serves as the central orchestration point for the entire determination process through flexible Determination Rules and its structured Access Sequence mechanism. 

Key attributes include: 

1. Enterprise Service Organization Profile 

  • Profile ID: Unique identifier for each profile. 
  • Determination Rule: Defines the data sources and sequence used to determine the enterprise service organization through condition-based configuration. 
  • Auto-Redetermination: Enables automatic redetermination when underlying data changes. 
  • Mandatory Field Control: Option to make Enterprise Service Organization a required field in service transactions. 

2. Determination Rules and Conditions 

The system uses a prioritized access sequence that evaluates each determination step along with the configured data sources.  

  • Sales Area-Based: Leverages existing customizing table for backward compatibility (mapping between the Sales Area and the ESO). 
  • Service Team's Attribute-Based: Uses the service team from the transaction header to retrieve the maintained value of the enterprise service organization from the team’s attribute. 
  • Custom BAdI Logic: Allows implementation of complex, organization-specific determination rules. 

Each access step includes a Conclusive Indicator that controls whether the system should continue evaluating next steps or stop once a result has been found.  

Conclusive Indicator in details: 

The system determines the results from the conditions sequentially. The system will stop determination when it reaches the condition for which the conclusive indicator has been enabled and will accumulate all the results until this step. However, if no results have been determined, the system will continue evaluating the subsequent conditions irrespective of the conclusive indicator being enabled. If the conclusive indicator is not selected, the system will evaluate all conditions and will accumulate all the results. 

Simple scenarios with sample values:  

Scenario 

Step 

Determined Value 

Conclusive Indicator 

Behaviour 

Final Result 

1.1. Conclusive on the first condition

010 

 EntSrvcOrg1 

Enabled 

Processing stops at 010 since a result was found, and it is conclusive.

EntSrvcOrg1 

2.1. 010 returns nothing, and 020 is conclusive 

010 

Nothing 

Disabled 

No result, so the evaluation continues to the next step.

 

2.2. 010 returns nothing, and 020 is conclusive 

020 

 EntSrvcOrg2 

Enabled 

Result found at 020, and it is conclusive; processing stops.. 

EntSrvcOrg2 

3.1. No conclusive indicators on any condition  

010 

 EntSrvcOrg1 

Disabled 

Continue evaluation. 

 

 3.2. No conclusive indicators on any condition  

020 

 EntSrvcOrg2 

Disabled 

Continue evaluation. 

 

3.3. No conclusive indicators on any condition   

030 

 EntSrvcOrg3 

Disabled 

All steps evaluated; all results accumulated. 

EntSrvcOrg1, EntSrvcOrg2, EntSrvcOrg3 

4.1. Conclusive on 020, with result already from 010 

010 

 EntSrvcOrg1 

Disabled 

Continue evaluation. 

 

 4.2. Conclusive on 020, with result already from 010 

020 

 EntSrvcOrg2 

Enabled 

Stop after 020, collecting results from 010 and 020. 

EntSrvcOrg1, EntSrvcOrg2 

Notes: 

  • If a condition with a conclusive indicator is reached but no result has been determined yet, the system will continue evaluating the next conditions (i.e., the conclusive flag does not force stopping when there are no results so far). 
  • The exact enterprise service organizations you accumulate depend on which conditions return results and which conditions have the conclusive indicator enabled. 

Enhanced Features 

1. When multiple Enterprise Service Organizations are determined: 

  • UI Interactions: Users see a pop-up allowing selection from available options. 

Important Note: If the pop-up is closed without selecting a value, a navigational message will be logged, allowing the user to relaunch the pop-up.

  • API Integration and Background Processing: The system defaults to the first valid result or respects supplied values. 
  • Flexible Configuration: Conclusive indicators control result accumulation across determination steps. 

2. Auto-Redetermination Capability: 

The system can automatically redetermine Enterprise Service Organizations when: 

  • Sales area information changes. 
  • Service team assignments are modified. 
  • Custom BAdI logic indicates relevance for redetermination. 
  • Only applies to automatically determined values, preserving manual entries. 

Steps to Enable Enterprise Service Organization Determination

To leverage this enhanced functionality, configure the following customization settings in the system: 

1. Enable Enterprise Organizational Model in your system configuration 

SIMG > Service > Basic Functions > Enterprise Organizational Management > Enable Enterprise Organizational Model 

2. Define Enterprise Service Organization Determination Rule 

SIMG > Service > Basic Functions > Enterprise Organizational Management > Define Enterprise Service Organization Determination Rule 

  • Define Determination Rule based on your business requirements 

  • Assign Conditions with appropriate sequence steps and data sources  

3. Define Determination Profiles based on your business requirements 

SIMG > Service > Basic Functions > Enterprise Organizational Management > Define Enterprise Service Organization Profile 

4. Assign Profile to relevant process/transaction type 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Define Transaction Types 

Conclusion 

The Enhanced Enterprise Service Organization Determination feature represents a significant step forward in SAP S/4HANA’s service management capabilities. By providing flexible, rule-based determination logic while maintaining seamless compatibility with existing processes, this enhancement empowers organizations to implement sophisticated service organization assignment strategies that align perfectly with their unique business requirements. 

This feature is now available in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01, ready to transform how your organization manages service transactions and organizational assignments. 

For detailed implementation guidance and technical specifications, please consult the official SAP help documentation and engage with your SAP implementation partner to ensure an optimal configuration for your specific business needs.

entry.title: RAP Fiori Application Works in ADT Preview but Not Correctly in Fiori Launchpad (SAP Public cloud)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have developed an RAP Excel Upload application using RAP in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using ABAP Development Tools (ADT Eclipse) only, without using SAP Business Application Studio.

WHAT IS WORKING

In ADT Eclipse, when I click Preview on the Service Binding, the application works perfectly.

All features are working correctly:

  • Create upload record
  • Upload Excel file
  • Download template
  • Process Excel
  • Header data is visible
  • Item data is visible
  • Navigation between Header and Item levels works correctly

    WHAT I DID

    1. Created the Fiori application using Quick Fiori Application Generator in ADT.
    2. Transported the objects to the Test system.
    3. Created:
      • IAM App
      • Business Catalog
    4. Assigned the catalog to the required business role.

    Now the application appears in the Fiori Launchpad.


    ISSUE

    Although the application opens in the Fiori Launchpad, it does not behave the same way as in ADT Preview.

    In ADT Preview, I can see the navigation arrow (>) to expand and navigate between Header and Item tables.

    However, in the Fiori Launchpad version, the navigation arrow is not visible, so I cannot navigate from Header to Item records.

    Because of this, the application is not functioning as expected.

    Screenshot

    Below is the screen where the Fiori app was generated in ADT.

    This screen shot is from the ADT preview here it is working correctly



    This one is from the Fiori Launchpad which is not working correctly

     

     



    Question

    Could someone please help me understand:

    • Why the navigation arrow is visible in ADT Preview but not in Fiori Launchpad?
    • Is there any additional configuration required for RAP associations or Fiori elements navigation in S/4HANA Public Cloud?

    Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

    Thank you!

entry.title: Re: What contains under unused_data of disk_overview query
entry.content:
Hi Ameya,

"UNUSED_GB" is basically the difference between TOTAL_GB and the USED_GB size of the allocated space assigned by the hana/data volume.
FRAG_PCT represents this difference.

As explained in the header of the 'HANA_Disks_Overview.txt' script in Note https://me.sap.com/notes/1969700

- TOTAL_GB: Total space (GB)
- USED_GB: Used space (GB)
- UNUSED_GB: Unused space (GB)
- FRAG_PCT: Fragmentation (%), unused space vs. total space


Kind Regards
Simon

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 - Edição dos 10 anos do SITREC
entry.content:
Inscrições em breve

 

Celebrando 10 anos do SAP Inside Track Recife, desde sua primeira edição em 2016, reforçamos a força de uma comunidade que cresce com colaboração, troca de conhecimento e conexões genuínas no Nordeste. Inserido em uma jornada nacional iniciada em 2009, o evento percorreu diversas cidades, como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Curitiba, Porto Alegre, Belo Horizonte, Fortaleza, Salvador, Campinas, Ribeirão Preto e São José dos Campos, consolidando o SAP Inside Track como um movimento que vai além da tecnologia, é sobre pessoas, experiências e a construção contínua de um ecossistema SAP cada vez mais forte no Brasil.

Veja como foi a ultima edição ano passado: Blog SITREC2025

Acompanhe palestras anteriores aqui neste link: SAP Inside Track Recife - Playlist


A agenda do evento 2026 ainda não foi divulgada.

O Que Esperar do Evento

O SAP Inside Track Recife é a maior oportunidade de aprender, compartilhar experiências e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP. Teremos palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando as mais diversas soluções SAP, incluindo as inovações do SAP S/4HANA.

Além disso, este é o momento perfeito para expandir seu networking e conhecer pessoalmente grandes profissionais da Comunidade SAP e das redes sociais.

Veja como foram as edições passadas neste link -> #SITREC

Data: 23/05/2026

Local: Hotel Manibu Recife - Avenida Conselheiro Aguiar - Boa Viagem, Recife - PE, Brasil

Inscrição: Não liberada (Inscrições para aquisição de ingressos no Eventbrite)

Chamada para Palestrantes

Se você tem conhecimento para compartilhar, esta é sua oportunidade! Envie sua proposta com o tema, título e uma sinopse para: sapinsidetrackbrazil@gmail.com  o tempo de palestra é de no máximo 40 minutos, e o último dia para envio de propostas é em 05 de Agosto de 2024. Aguarde o contato da nossa equipe com a confirmação.

Agenda: A definir

Sobre Recife

Recife, a capital do estado de Pernambuco, carrega em seu nome e história a essência das características geográficas e culturais que marcam a região. A palavra "Recife" deriva do termo árabe "rasif", que significa "cais" ou "quebra-mar", uma referência direta à formação natural dos arrecifes de coral que protegem sua costa e criam as famosas piscinas naturais. Por sua vez, o nome "Pernambuco" tem origem no tupi, com as palavras "paranã-puka" ou "para-nã-mbuk", traduzidas como "mar furado" ou "buraco no mar", também referenciando as passagens naturais existentes nos arrecifes. Tanto Recife quanto Pernambuco refletem, em suas etimologias, uma conexão intrínseca com o mar e com as formações naturais que moldaram não apenas sua geografia, mas também sua identidade cultural.

Hoje, Recife é uma das cidades mais antigas do Brasil e se destaca como a quarta mais desenvolvida entre os mercados emergentes globais. A capital abriga o Porto Digital, o maior parque tecnológico do Brasil, e possui o segundo maior polo médico do país, consolidando-se como um centro de inovação e excelência em saúde. Seu aeroporto internacional, reconhecido por sua qualidade, conecta a cidade ao mundo, enquanto sua rica cultura gastronômica, fortemente influenciada por séculos de miscigenação, atrai visitantes de todas as partes.

Combinando uma infraestrutura de ponta com uma vibrante cena cultural e tecnológica, Recife é muito mais do que uma cidade: é um símbolo de resiliência e inovação. A cidade, assim como o estado de Pernambuco, que a abriga, mantém vivas as marcas de sua história enquanto projeta um futuro de oportunidades, consolidando-se como um destino privilegiado para negócios, turismo e eventos de relevância nacional e internacional.

Organizadores e Patrocínio

O SAP Inside Track Recife é organizado por membros dedicados da Comunidade SAP, com apoio de diversos patrocinadores que tornam este evento possível. Gostaríamos de agradecer a todos que contribuem para o sucesso deste encontro.

Apoio: A definir

Junte-se a nós no SAP Inside Track Recife 2026!

Esperamos vê-lo lá para um dia repleto de aprendizado, networking e inovação.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Plus - Pune (March 2026) Recap
entry.content:
What started as a plan to bring the community back together turned into a morning full of ideas, questions, and some very honest conversations around AI in the SAP space. Safe to say, the energy in the room did not disappoint! 🙌

A quick look at what we covered:

• From AI Experiments to an Enterprise AI Operating System
A strong reminder that AI success is not about more models, but better data and clearer foundations. Moving from experiments to real enterprise adoption needs structure.

• AI-Powered Extensions on SAP BTP
A very real example of cleaning and optimizing large-scale master data using a multi-agent approach. If there was one takeaway here, it’s this: bad data still causes most problems.

• ITOps Copilot
AI stepping into IT operations with LLM-driven ticket analysis. Faster insights, less manual effort, and a glimpse into how support teams might work going forward.

• SAP Document AI in Action
Invoices going from paper to structured SAP entries with minimal human intervention. Simple idea, big impact.

• The Future of Intelligent ERP
AI, Joule, and the “right data, right process, right time” approach shaping what the next generation of ERP could look like.

But beyond the sessions, what really stood out was the community itself. Conversations that continued between sessions, people exchanging notes, and a room that stayed engaged throughout.

Also, putting this together was just as much fun as attending it. Always a good sign! 💪

A big thank you to our speakers, participants, and everyone who helped make this happen behind the scenes.

We’re just getting started with Stammtisch Plus in Pune. More to come! Stay tuned!

Scroll for some glimpses of the event!

Organizers & Volunteers:

  • @Parineeta_Somvanshi 
  • @Punith_Oswal 
  • @Asutosh21 

#StammPune

Best Regards,
SAP Community Group – Pune 


Our Speakers!Our Volunteers!

entry.title: ZBV / CUA is not working as expected
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm currently having a strange issue with the CUA. Everything is working perfectly in our CUA system. We don't have duplicate company addresses, and users are assigned the correct one. Our Q-system is also working as expected. We are currently on S4/HANA 2023 SP 03/2025.

But here’s the problem: We’re currently having issues with our P-system. In this system, there are several users assigned to a different company; for example, the correct company address would be 41000 – Standard Company, and now we have about 90 addressesSAP with a new number and a new description -> 41001 – Standard Company0000012345, etc.

We have deleted or corrected these incorrect company addresses in the CUA system, the Dev system, and the Q system, but we cannot delete them in the P system. Do you have any idea how we can delete these incorrect entries? I tried using transaction SUCOMP, but it doesn’t work. I receive the following error message when I try to delete a company address, and the address we want to use is the default address:

And even if I assign a new company to the user, I still can't delete that incorrect company.
Sincerely, Dominik 🙂
